exercises.prompt: The tournament committee is auditing which archetypes draw the most restriction pressure on the TCG banlist. Working only from TCG banlist entries (banlist.format = 'tcg') joined to their card's archetype — ignore cards whose archetype is NULL — report every archetype with at least 3 TCG banlist entries. For each, return archetype, forbidden_count (its entries with status 'Forbidden'), limited_count (status 'Limited'), semi_count (status 'Semi-Limited'), total_entries (all its TCG entries), and pressure_rank = the archetype's rank by total_entries descending using RANK() so tied archetypes share a rank. Order by total_entries descending, then archetype ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: WITH tcg_bans AS (SELECT c.archetype, b.status FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL), by_arch AS (SELECT archetype, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Forbidden' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS forbidden_count, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Limited' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS limited_count, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Semi-Limited' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS semi_count, COUNT(*) AS total_entries FROM tcg_bans GROUP BY archetype) SELECT archetype, forbidden_count, limited_count, semi_count, total_entries, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_entries DESC) AS pressure_rank FROM by_arch WHERE total_entries >= 3 ORDER BY total_entries DESC, archetype
hints: Two steps. First a CTE (call it tcg_bans) that joins cards to banlist, keeps only format 'tcg' rows with a non-null archetype, and carries archetype and status. Then group that by archetype: use SUM(CASE WHEN status = '...' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) for each of the three status counts, COUNT(*) for total_entries, and RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_entries DESC) for the rank. Filter to total_entries >= 3 and order as asked.

WITH tcg_bans AS (SELECT c.archetype, b.status FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON ... WHERE ... AND ...), by_arch AS (SELECT archetype, SUM(CASE WHEN ... THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS forbidden_count, SUM(CASE WHEN ... THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS limited_count, SUM(CASE WHEN ... THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS semi_count, COUNT(*) AS total_entries FROM tcg_bans GROUP BY ...) SELECT archetype, forbidden_count, limited_count, semi_count, total_entries, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY ...) AS pressure_rank FROM by_arch WHERE ... ORDER BY ....

```sql
WITH tcg_bans AS (SELECT c.archetype, b.status FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL), by_arch AS (SELECT archetype, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Forbidden' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS forbidden_count, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Limited' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS limited_count, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Semi-Limited' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS semi_count, COUNT(*) AS total_entries FROM tcg_bans GROUP BY archetype) SELECT archetype, forbidden_count, limited_count, semi_count, total_entries, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_entries DESC) AS pressure_rank FROM by_arch WHERE total_entries >= 3 ORDER BY total_entries DESC, archetype
```

exercises.prompt: The committee suspects some cards are punished far harder in the TCG than in the OCG and wants them flagged. Score each banlist entry by severity — 'Forbidden' = 3, 'Limited' = 2, 'Semi-Limited' = 1 — and treat a card absent from a format's list as severity 0. Return every card that carries a TCG banlist entry AND whose TCG severity is strictly greater than its OCG severity. For each, return name, tcg_status (its TCG status), ocg_status (its OCG status, or 'Unrestricted' if it has none), and severity_gap (TCG severity minus OCG severity). Order by severity_gap descending, then name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: WITH sev AS (SELECT card_id, format, status, CASE status WHEN 'Forbidden' THEN 3 WHEN 'Limited' THEN 2 WHEN 'Semi-Limited' THEN 1 END AS score FROM banlist), tcg AS (SELECT card_id, score, status FROM sev WHERE format = 'tcg'), ocg AS (SELECT card_id, score, status FROM sev WHERE format = 'ocg') SELECT c.name, COALESCE(t.status, 'Unrestricted') AS tcg_status, COALESCE(o.status, 'Unrestricted') AS ocg_status, COALESCE(t.score, 0) - COALESCE(o.score, 0) AS severity_gap FROM cards c JOIN tcg t ON c.id = t.card_id LEFT JOIN ocg o ON c.id = o.card_id WHERE COALESCE(t.score, 0) > COALESCE(o.score, 0) ORDER BY severity_gap DESC, c.name
hints: Build a severity CTE over banlist that maps status to a 3/2/1 score with CASE, then split it into a tcg CTE and an ocg CTE (each filtered by format). Start from cards, INNER JOIN the tcg CTE (keeping only cards on the TCG list) and LEFT JOIN the ocg CTE (a card may be absent from the OCG list, so its ocg score and status come back NULL). COALESCE the missing OCG score to 0 and status to 'Unrestricted', then keep rows where the TCG score beats the OCG score.

WITH sev AS (SELECT card_id, format, status, CASE status WHEN ... THEN 3 WHEN ... THEN 2 WHEN ... THEN 1 END AS score FROM banlist), tcg AS (SELECT card_id, score, status FROM sev WHERE ...), ocg AS (SELECT card_id, score, status FROM sev WHERE ...) SELECT c.name, COALESCE(t.status, ...) AS tcg_status, COALESCE(o.status, ...) AS ocg_status, COALESCE(t.score, 0) - COALESCE(o.score, 0) AS severity_gap FROM cards c JOIN tcg t ON ... LEFT JOIN ocg o ON ... WHERE ... > ... ORDER BY ....

```sql
WITH sev AS (SELECT card_id, format, status, CASE status WHEN 'Forbidden' THEN 3 WHEN 'Limited' THEN 2 WHEN 'Semi-Limited' THEN 1 END AS score FROM banlist), tcg AS (SELECT card_id, score, status FROM sev WHERE format = 'tcg'), ocg AS (SELECT card_id, score, status FROM sev WHERE format = 'ocg') SELECT c.name, COALESCE(t.status, 'Unrestricted') AS tcg_status, COALESCE(o.status, 'Unrestricted') AS ocg_status, COALESCE(t.score, 0) - COALESCE(o.score, 0) AS severity_gap FROM cards c JOIN tcg t ON c.id = t.card_id LEFT JOIN ocg o ON c.id = o.card_id WHERE COALESCE(t.score, 0) > COALESCE(o.score, 0) ORDER BY severity_gap DESC, c.name
```

exercises.prompt: For a power-level briefing, the committee wants the top attackers in three key monster types. Among cards with a non-null atk whose race is 'Dragon', 'Spellcaster', or 'Warrior', take the top 3 by atk within each race, breaking atk ties by name ascending. Return race, name, atk, race_top (the highest atk in that race), and gap_to_top (race_top minus this card's atk). Order the result by race ascending, then atk descending, then name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: WITH pool AS (SELECT name, race, atk FROM cards WHERE atk IS NOT NULL AND race IN ('Dragon', 'Spellcaster', 'Warrior')), ranked AS (SELECT name, race, atk, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY race ORDER BY atk DESC, name) AS rn, MAX(atk) OVER (PARTITION BY race) AS race_top FROM pool) SELECT race, name, atk, race_top, race_top - atk AS gap_to_top FROM ranked WHERE rn <= 3 ORDER BY race, atk DESC, name
hints: First a CTE (pool) that filters cards to a non-null atk and the three named races. Then a CTE (ranked) that adds two windows partitioned by race: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY race ORDER BY atk DESC, name) to number contenders, and MAX(atk) OVER (PARTITION BY race) to carry each race's top atk onto every row. The outer query keeps the rows numbered 3 or lower and computes gap_to_top as race_top - atk.

WITH pool AS (SELECT name, race, atk FROM cards WHERE ... AND race IN (...)), ranked AS (SELECT name, race, atk, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ...) AS rn, MAX(atk) OVER (PARTITION BY ...) AS race_top FROM pool) SELECT race, name, atk, race_top, ... AS gap_to_top FROM ranked WHERE rn <= ... ORDER BY ....

```sql
WITH pool AS (SELECT name, race, atk FROM cards WHERE atk IS NOT NULL AND race IN ('Dragon', 'Spellcaster', 'Warrior')), ranked AS (SELECT name, race, atk, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY race ORDER BY atk DESC, name) AS rn, MAX(atk) OVER (PARTITION BY race) AS race_top FROM pool) SELECT race, name, atk, race_top, race_top - atk AS gap_to_top FROM ranked WHERE rn <= 3 ORDER BY race, atk DESC, name
```

exercises.prompt: The reprint desk wants to know which cards flood the print runs. Join cards to card_sets and, for each card with at least 25 printings (rows in card_sets), return name, type, printing_count (number of printings), rarity_count (COUNT of DISTINCT rarity across those printings), reprint_tier (CASE: 'Staple' when printing_count >= 40, 'Common Reprint' when printing_count >= 30, otherwise 'Frequent'), and reprint_rank = RANK() OVER (ORDER BY printing_count DESC) with tied cards sharing a rank. Order by printing_count descending, then name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: WITH prints AS (SELECT c.id, c.name, c.type, COUNT(*) AS printing_count, COUNT(DISTINCT cs.rarity) AS rarity_count FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name, c.type) SELECT name, type, printing_count, rarity_count, CASE WHEN printing_count >= 40 THEN 'Staple' WHEN printing_count >= 30 THEN 'Common Reprint' ELSE 'Frequent' END AS reprint_tier, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY printing_count DESC) AS reprint_rank FROM prints WHERE printing_count >= 25 ORDER BY printing_count DESC, name
hints: One CTE does the heavy lifting: join cards to card_sets and GROUP BY the card (its id, name, and type) so COUNT(*) is the printing_count and COUNT(DISTINCT cs.rarity) is the rarity_count. The outer query filters to printing_count >= 25, assigns the tier with a CASE ladder, and ranks with RANK() OVER (ORDER BY printing_count DESC).

WITH prints AS (SELECT c.id, c.name, c.type, COUNT(*) AS printing_count, COUNT(DISTINCT ...) AS rarity_count FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON ... GROUP BY c.id, c.name, c.type) SELECT name, type, printing_count, rarity_count, CASE WHEN ... THEN 'Staple' WHEN ... THEN 'Common Reprint' ELSE ... END AS reprint_tier, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY ...) AS reprint_rank FROM prints WHERE ... ORDER BY ....

```sql
WITH prints AS (SELECT c.id, c.name, c.type, COUNT(*) AS printing_count, COUNT(DISTINCT cs.rarity) AS rarity_count FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name, c.type) SELECT name, type, printing_count, rarity_count, CASE WHEN printing_count >= 40 THEN 'Staple' WHEN printing_count >= 30 THEN 'Common Reprint' ELSE 'Frequent' END AS reprint_tier, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY printing_count DESC) AS reprint_rank FROM prints WHERE printing_count >= 25 ORDER BY printing_count DESC, name
```

exercises.prompt: For the committee's final dossier, identify the 'dangerous archetypes' and rank them by raw power. An archetype qualifies when it has at least 20 cards total AND at least one card that is 'Forbidden' in the TCG format. For each qualifying archetype return archetype, card_count (its total number of cards), avg_atk (the average atk over its cards that have a non-null atk, rounded to 1 decimal), max_atk (its highest atk), and power_rank = RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_atk DESC) with ties sharing a rank. Order by avg_atk descending, then archetype ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: WITH arch_size AS (SELECT archetype, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards WHERE archetype IS NOT NULL GROUP BY archetype HAVING COUNT(*) >= 20), forbidden_arch AS (SELECT DISTINCT c.archetype FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL), arch_power AS (SELECT archetype, ROUND(AVG(atk), 1) AS avg_atk, MAX(atk) AS max_atk FROM cards WHERE archetype IS NOT NULL AND atk IS NOT NULL GROUP BY archetype) SELECT s.archetype, s.card_count, p.avg_atk, p.max_atk, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY p.avg_atk DESC) AS power_rank FROM arch_size s JOIN forbidden_arch f ON s.archetype = f.archetype JOIN arch_power p ON s.archetype = p.archetype ORDER BY p.avg_atk DESC, s.archetype
hints: Three CTEs, one per condition, then join them. arch_size: COUNT(*) per archetype with HAVING COUNT(*) >= 20. forbidden_arch: SELECT DISTINCT archetype from cards joined to banlist where format 'tcg' and status 'Forbidden'. arch_power: per-archetype ROUND(AVG(atk), 1) and MAX(atk) over cards with a non-null atk. The final query joins arch_size to forbidden_arch (an inner join enforces the 'has a forbidden card' rule) and to arch_power, then ranks by avg_atk.

WITH arch_size AS (SELECT archetype, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards WHERE ... GROUP BY archetype HAVING ...), forbidden_arch AS (SELECT DISTINCT c.archetype FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON ... WHERE ... AND ... AND ...), arch_power AS (SELECT archetype, ROUND(AVG(atk), 1) AS avg_atk, MAX(atk) AS max_atk FROM cards WHERE ... GROUP BY archetype) SELECT s.archetype, s.card_count, p.avg_atk, p.max_atk, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY ...) AS power_rank FROM arch_size s JOIN forbidden_arch f ON ... JOIN arch_power p ON ... ORDER BY ....

```sql
WITH arch_size AS (SELECT archetype, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards WHERE archetype IS NOT NULL GROUP BY archetype HAVING COUNT(*) >= 20), forbidden_arch AS (SELECT DISTINCT c.archetype FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL), arch_power AS (SELECT archetype, ROUND(AVG(atk), 1) AS avg_atk, MAX(atk) AS max_atk FROM cards WHERE archetype IS NOT NULL AND atk IS NOT NULL GROUP BY archetype) SELECT s.archetype, s.card_count, p.avg_atk, p.max_atk, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY p.avg_atk DESC) AS power_rank FROM arch_size s JOIN forbidden_arch f ON s.archetype = f.archetype JOIN arch_power p ON s.archetype = p.archetype ORDER BY p.avg_atk DESC, s.archetype
```
